response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable engineer with a strong software craftsmanship background and genuine interest in developer tooling, but his core technical profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary stack across the last decade is TypeScript, Node.js, and D-lang — Java appears only peripherally (robotic arms at Altom, brief mention at NI) and Kotlin is entirely absent. There is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework work, and while his NI LabWindows role touched an IDE product, it was a C IDE with no JVM internals relevance. His test framework and MCP tooling work shows tooling aptitude, but it does not bridge the gap to the deep JVM and enterprise Java expertise this role requires.
strengths: Demonstrated tooling and developer-experience focus via open-source projects (Fluent-Asserts, Trial test runner, MCP code generators)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and pairing applied consistently across roles
Prior exposure to IDE-adjacent product work at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI C IDE)
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin production experience; primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and D-lang across all recent and senior roles
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience anywhere in the resume
No JVM tooling or IDE internals work; the NI IDE role was C-based and brief (6 months), not indicative of JVM depth

key_factors.factor: No Spring or JVM enterprise framework experience
